jokes.text: D'oh! [Screams]
jokes.setup: Homer crashes his car in the opening sequence
jokes.punchline: His signature frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfectly character-specific and economical

jokes.text: Close, but no doughnut, cops.
jokes.setup: Police are at the wrong house looking for a cattle rustler
jokes.punchline: Homer's food-obsessed twist on 'close but no cigar'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer wordplay that reflects his food obsession while being economical and clever

jokes.text: This is Papa Bear. Put out an A.P.B. for a male suspect... driving a... car of some sort... heading in the direction of, uh... you know, that place that sells chili. Suspect is hatless. Repeat, hatless.
jokes.setup: Police chief giving an APB for a suspect
jokes.punchline: Completely vague description ending with the irrelevant detail that suspect is 'hatless'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum incompetence - builds absurdity through specificity in all the wrong places, ending with the meaningless but emphatic 'hatless'

jokes.text: My heart is just f-- [Gasps, Groans] - Homie, what is it? -Just workin' the turkey through.
jokes.setup: Homer dismisses heart concerns while eating in bed
jokes.punchline: Has what appears to be a heart attack but claims he's just digesting turkey
jokes.explanation: Dark comedy establishing Homer's denial about his health while maintaining his gluttonous character

jokes.text: Hey, Lise, there was a big train wreck last night. Do you wanna see the victims? - Hmm, okay. - Yaah! - Bart, that's gross! - You're right. Let's bury them at sea. Bleah!
jokes.setup: Bart offers to show Lisa train wreck victims
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just toy trains, then decides to 'bury them at sea' in what's presumably the toilet
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart mischief with dark humor misdirection - sets up morbid expectation then reveals innocent prank

jokes.text: You know that feeling you get... when a thousand knives of fire are stabbing you in the heart? I got that right now. Ooh! Bacon!
jokes.setup: Homer describes severe chest pain in dramatic terms
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets distracted by bacon despite describing heart attack symptoms
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - genuine concern immediately undermined by food obsession, showing his priorities

jokes.text: Psst! The best meat's in the rump.
jokes.setup: Marge serves Homer healthy oatmeal instead of meat
jokes.punchline: Homer whispers meat advice as if it's a conspiracy
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's meat obsession and his conspiratorial approach to food

jokes.text: What the hell is this? - Nice, healthy oatmeal. - Ooh, oatmeal. What a delightful treat. Oh, there's a bug in it. - No, there isn't. - Trust me.
jokes.setup: Homer reacts negatively to healthy oatmeal then pretends to be enthusiastic
jokes.punchline: Claims there's a bug to avoid eating it, Marge denies it, Homer insists 'trust me'
jokes.explanation: Homer's transparent attempt to avoid healthy food through fake enthusiasm then excuse-making

jokes.text: Dad, there's a bug on that. Eh.
jokes.setup: Lisa points out there actually is a bug on Homer's food
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual 'Eh' and presumably eats it anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy - Homer's complete lack of food standards captured in one dismissive syllable

jokes.text: Whew! I was afraid it was my transmission.
jokes.setup: Told his heart is failing and making irregular thumping
jokes.punchline: Relief that it's his heart and not his car transmission
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Homer values his car more than his health, perfectly delivered with genuine relief

jokes.text: Billy, remember that old Plymouth We just couldn't fix? - We're gonna sell him to Mr. Nikapopolus? - You're a dull boy, Billy.
jokes.setup: Mechanics discussing what to do with Homer after his heart diagnosis
jokes.punchline: Comparing him to an unfixable car they'll sell to an unsuspecting buyer
jokes.explanation: Dark humor treating Homer like a broken-down car, with the dismissive 'dull boy' adding insult

jokes.text: That's right. Keep eating. Little do you know you're drawing ever closer to the poisoned doughnut. [Chuckling] There is a poisoned one-- isn't there, Smithers? Uh, no, sir. I discussed this with our lawyers. They consider it murder. Damn their oily hides!
jokes.setup: Burns watching Homer eat, plotting with a poisoned donut
jokes.punchline: Lawyers preventing murder, Burns cursing their 'oily hides' in frustration
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns evil thwarted by legal technicalities, with characteristically odd insult about lawyers

jokes.text: Damn their oily hides!
jokes.setup: Burns frustrated by lawyers preventing murder
jokes.punchline: Old-timey curse about lawyers having 'oily hides'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns antiquated language and imagery for cursing lawyers

jokes.text: Relax, Simpson. I just brought you in here for a friendly hello. [Sighs] And good-bye! You're fired! [Gasps] But wait. Perhaps I'm being Too hasty. You are highly skilled... [Sighs] at goofing off!
jokes.setup: Burns seeming to be friendly, then firing Homer, then reconsidering
jokes.punchline: The 'skill' is goofing off, delivered with fake praise structure
jokes.explanation: Well-structured misdirection joke with Burns' characteristic false praise format

jokes.text: Now, don't worry, Homer. You're the kind of guy I could really dig... a grave for! [Gasps] Your indolence is inefficacious! Oh? That means you're terrible!
jokes.setup: Burns appearing to compliment Homer with 'dig'
jokes.punchline: Reveals he means dig a grave, then uses big words Homer doesn't understand
jokes.explanation: Double punchline - morbid wordplay followed by Burns' vocabulary being too advanced for Homer

jokes.text: Mr. Burns, I think he's dead. Oh, dear. Send a ham to his widow. Mmm. Ham. No, wait. He's alive. Oh, good. Cancel the ham. D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer appears to have died from the stress
jokes.punchline: Burns' casual ham gift, Homer's unconscious 'Mmm ham', then disappointment when ham is cancelled
jokes.explanation: Perfectly orchestrated sequence - Burns' casual attitude toward death, Homer's unconscious food response, then frustration

jokes.text: He was taking a bite, and his jaw locked. Hey, look. I can fit my entire fist in here. [Muffled] Hey, hey, hey. Cut it out. What's that, Chief? Cut it out.
jokes.setup: Homer's jaw is locked from his heart attack
jokes.punchline: Wiggum sticks his fist in Homer's mouth, can't understand Homer's muffled protests
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy with Wiggum's characteristic obliviousness to others' discomfort

jokes.text: Ooh. Buy three tubes of Mr. Blister... get one free. [Phone Ringing] Hello. Yes. Oh, dear Lord! Homer's in the hospital! They think it's his heart! [Door Shuts] Oh, my God. What? Five cents off wax paper.
jokes.setup: Marge gets devastating news about Homer's hospitalization
jokes.punchline: Her 'Oh my God' is actually about a coupon deal, not Homer
jokes.explanation: Effective misdirection - sets up dramatic reaction, reveals it's about mundane savings

jokes.text: Marge, my whole life flashed before my eyes. [Flashback sequence of Homer as a child with angelic voice that cracks] What a voice. Young Homer is going to make me millionaire. [Voice cracks] Hey, my voice just changed. [Singing tunelessly] Dagnabbit!
jokes.setup: Homer's life flashing before his eyes in near-death experience
jokes.punchline: Flashback shows his brief moment of having a beautiful voice before puberty ruined it
jokes.explanation: Creative backstory gag explaining Homer's lack of musical talent through cruel timing of puberty

jokes.text: What a voice. Young Homer is going to make me a millionaire. [Voice Cracks, Deepens] Hey, my voice just changed. [Singing Tunelessly] Dagnabbit!
jokes.setup: Manager excited about young Homer's singing voice
jokes.punchline: Homer's voice cracks mid-sentence, ruining his career prospects
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing of puberty ruining Homer's one chance at success

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, I'm afraid you've just had a mild heart attack. But I'm out of the woods now, right? I mean, whatever doesn't kill me can only make me stronger. Oh, no. Quite the opposite. It's made you weak as a kitten.
jokes.setup: Homer optimistically quotes the 'what doesn't kill you makes you stronger' cliché
jokes.punchline: Doctor completely contradicts this with medical reality
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of common inspirational phrase with medical reality check

jokes.text: Look! Hey, come on. Quit it. Coochy coochy coo! Stop. Please stop. Oh, you swing like a girl. Come on, now. Please have mercy. Got your nose. Not funny.
jokes.setup: Doctor demonstrating Homer's weakness
jokes.punchline: Treating grown Homer like a baby with tickling and baby talk
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy from doctor's unprofessional behavior and Homer's helplessness

jokes.text: How 'bout this little bee? [Buzzing] No! Oh! Remember your hippopotamus oath.
jokes.setup: Doctor continuing to torment weakened Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer mangles 'Hippocratic oath' as 'hippopotamus oath'
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer malapropism showing his ignorance while being tortured

jokes.text: Remember your hippopotamus oath.
jokes.setup: Homer appealing to doctor's medical ethics
jokes.punchline: Mispronounces 'Hippocratic oath' as 'hippopotamus oath'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer malapropism - shows his ignorance while being inherently funny word substitution

jokes.text: Well, we can't fix his heart... but we can tell you exactly how damaged it is. What an age we live in!
jokes.setup: Inability to help Homer medically
jokes.punchline: Enthusiastic celebration of diagnostic technology despite being unable to treat
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of modern medicine's emphasis on diagnosis over treatment

jokes.text: Now, Homer, this is a new body fat analysis test. I start you jiggling and measure how long it takes to stop. Whoo-hoo! Look at that blubber fly! Yes. Nurse, cancel my 1:00.
jokes.setup: Doctor explaining a medical test for body fat
jokes.punchline: Doctor gets distracted and entertained by Homer's jiggling fat
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy with doctor's unprofessional fascination with the test results

jokes.text: Say it in English, Doc. You're going to need open-heart surgery. Spare me your medical mumbo jumbo. We're going to cut you open and tinker with your ticker. Could you dumb it down a shade?
jokes.setup: Homer asking for simpler medical explanations
jokes.punchline: Each explanation gets simpler but Homer still needs it dumbed down more
jokes.explanation: Great escalating stupidity gag showing Homer's complete inability to understand even simple explanations

jokes.text: This procedure will cost you upwards of... $30,000. [Homer gasps, cries out, thud] I'm afraid it's now 40,000.
jokes.setup: Doctor quotes expensive surgery cost, Homer faints
jokes.punchline: The price goes up because of the fainting incident
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about healthcare costs with perfect timing - the problem literally gets worse

jokes.text: Now, how much do we have in the checkbook? Seventy dollars. Have we deposited any $40,000 checks that haven't cleared yet? No.
jokes.setup: Needing $40,000 for surgery with only $70
jokes.punchline: Homer's delusional hope they might have forgotten depositing a huge check
jokes.explanation: Homer's financial delusion and desperate optimism in the face of reality

jokes.text: Don't worry, Marge. America's health care system is second only to Japan... Canada, Sweden, Great Britain-- well, all of Europe. But you can thank your lucky stars we don't live in Paraguay.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to reassure Marge about American healthcare
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly more countries with better healthcare, ending with obscure Paraguay comparison
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating structure that satirizes American healthcare while showing Homer's ignorance - perfectly builds and lands

jokes.text: Questions? Questions? Oh! My whole scheme down the-- I mean, ask away.
jokes.setup: Homer applying for health insurance after his heart attack
jokes.punchline: Nearly admits he's running a scheme before catching himself
jokes.explanation: Homer's transparent attempt at insurance fraud with terrible execution

jokes.text: Oh, I thought that said 'brain hemorrhages.'
jokes.setup: Caught changing '3' heart attacks to '0'
jokes.punchline: Claims he misread 'heart attacks' as 'brain hemorrhages'
jokes.explanation: Homer's pathetic excuse that makes no logical sense but fits his character

jokes.text: Must... sign... policy! Sir, I'm sorry. We can't insure you. I made an 'H.' No, that doesn't count. It's like an 'X.'
jokes.setup: Homer desperately trying to sign insurance papers while having a heart attack
jokes.punchline: His attempted signature is just an 'H' which doesn't count
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy of Homer's failing health interfering with bureaucracy

jokes.text: Oh, Doctor, I was in a wonderful place filled with fire and brimstone. And there were all these guys in red pajamas sticking pitchforks in my butt.
jokes.setup: Homer describing his near-death experience
jokes.punchline: Describes hell as 'wonderful' and demons as guys in 'red pajamas'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer interpretation of hell - his obliviousness makes the terrifying sound pleasant and mundane

jokes.text: Maybe I should buy one of those machines. [Groaning] Oh, yeah. That's the stuff.
jokes.setup: Homer considering a massage chair as medical treatment
jokes.punchline: His satisfied reaction to the massage chair
jokes.explanation: Homer's simple-minded solution to heart problems with obvious enjoyment

jokes.text: Now, I know I haven't been the best Christian. In fact, when you're up there 'blah, blah, blah-ing,' I'm usually doodling or mentally undressing the female parishioners.
jokes.setup: Homer praying for money in church
jokes.punchline: Admits to not paying attention and having inappropriate thoughts during sermons
jokes.explanation: Homer's brutally honest prayer that undermines itself - perfect character moment of inappropriate oversharing

jokes.text: Now, I know I haven't been the best Jew... but I rented Fiddler on the Roof, and I will watch it.
jokes.setup: Homer trying different religions for money
jokes.punchline: His minimal commitment to Judaism involves renting one musical
jokes.explanation: Homer's shallow understanding of religious conversion reduced to consuming media

jokes.text: Son, they call it a 'droodle.' Whoo-hoo! Look at it go!
jokes.setup: Homer received something from the rabbi
jokes.punchline: It's apparently just a simple toy that entertains Homer immensely
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike fascination with simple objects, easily distracted from serious problems

jokes.text: Hi, everybody! Are you looking for a way to slash the cost... of your medical expenses? I will perform any operation for 129.95. Come in for brain surgery... and receive a free Chinese finger trap.
jokes.setup: TV ad for discount medical services
jokes.punchline: Ridiculously low price for surgery with toy prizes
jokes.explanation: Dr. Nick's introduction perfectly establishes his character as dangerously incompetent but cheap

jokes.text: Come in for brain surgery... and receive a free Chinese finger trap. [Grunting]
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick advertising brain surgery special offer
jokes.punchline: Free Chinese finger trap that he's caught in while demonstrating
jokes.explanation: Visual gag showing Dr. Nick's incompetence - can't handle a simple toy

jokes.text: Call 1-600-DOCTORB. The 'B' is for 'bargain'!
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick's phone number for his medical services
jokes.punchline: Extra letter in phone number justified as standing for 'bargain'
jokes.explanation: Perfect joke construction - the absurd phone number gets a perfectly logical explanation that makes no sense. Incredibly quotable and economical.

jokes.text: Well, Marge, we could do worse. How? Uh, some dog could do the operation.
jokes.setup: Marge questioning how Dr. Nick could be worse than qualified doctors
jokes.punchline: Homer's illogical comparison to a dog performing surgery
jokes.explanation: Homer's absurd logic trying to make Dr. Nick seem reasonable by comparison

jokes.text: Nothing you say can upset us. We're the MTV generation. We feel neither highs nor lows. Really? What's it like? Eh.
jokes.setup: Bart claiming their generation is emotionally numb
jokes.punchline: Homer's curiosity and Bart's perfectly apathetic 'Eh' response
jokes.explanation: Smart generational satire with perfect delivery - Bart's response proves his own point

jokes.text: So the tiny Aorta fairies will take Mr. Leg Vein on a long trip... to get married to Princess Left Ventricle.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to explain heart surgery to his kids in simple terms
jokes.punchline: Creates elaborate fairy tale metaphor for bypass surgery
jokes.explanation: Creative and sweet way Homer tries to make scary surgery sound magical for his kids

jokes.text: Dad, are you trying to tell us you're getting a coronary artery bypass graft? Uh, yeah.
jokes.setup: Homer's elaborate fairy tale explanation of his surgery
jokes.punchline: Lisa immediately understands and uses proper medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Perfect contrast between Homer's childish explanation and Lisa's intelligence - completely undermines his effort

jokes.text: What if they botch it? I won't have a dad... for a while.
jokes.setup: Bart worried about his father's surgery going wrong
jokes.punchline: Casual 'for a while' suggests death is temporary
jokes.explanation: Dark humor from child's misunderstanding of death's permanence, delivered with perfect casualness

jokes.text: Kids. Kids. I'm not gonna die. That only happens to bad people. What about Abraham Lincoln? Uh-- He sold poisoned milk to schoolchildren.
jokes.setup: Homer claiming only bad people die, Lisa challenges with Lincoln
jokes.punchline: Homer invents absurd justification for Lincoln's assassination
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline with Homer's ridiculous historical revision to maintain his flawed logic

jokes.text: Bed goes up. Bed goes down. Bed goes up. Bed goes down.
jokes.setup: Homer in hospital bed with controls
jokes.punchline: Becomes mesmerized by the simple up/down motion
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike fascination with simple mechanical functions, becomes running gag

jokes.text: Well, if that don't put the 'dink' in co-inky-dink.
jokes.setup: Homer encountering Flanders in the hospital
jokes.punchline: Mangled version of 'coincidence' that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Homer's attempt at clever wordplay that completely fails but fits his character

jokes.text: I'm having a kidney and a lung removed. Who are you donating 'em to? First come, first serve.
jokes.setup: Flanders explaining his organ donation surgery
jokes.punchline: Treats major organ donation like a casual store policy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders moment - his extreme generosity treated with casual business logic

jokes.text: If I could give you my heart, Homer, I would. Shut up, Flanders.
jokes.setup: Flanders offering heartfelt support to Homer
jokes.punchline: Homer's rude dismissal of genuine kindness
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer/Flanders dynamic - kindness met with inexplicable hostility

jokes.text: Dr. Riviera. Dr. Nick Riviera. Please report to the coroner immediately. The coroner? I'm so sick of that guy.
jokes.setup: Hospital paging Dr. Nick to the coroner
jokes.punchline: Dr. Nick treats frequent coroner visits as an annoyance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy establishing Dr. Nick's terrible track record through his casual annoyance

jokes.text: Well, see you in the operating place. Where are the bodies? Oh, such a nice day. I think I'll go out the window. Ho!
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick leaving casually, others looking for bodies
jokes.punchline: Someone decides to exit through window with crash sound
jokes.explanation: Hospital chaos with casual attitude toward bodies and window exits

jokes.text: Oh, such a nice day. I think I'll go out the window. Ho! [Objects clattering]
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick deciding to leave through the window
jokes.punchline: Sound of him falling and crashing
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy establishing Dr. Nick's poor judgment and clumsiness

jokes.text: Dear Lord, thank you For Ziggy comics, little baby ducks... and Sweatin' to the Oldies, volumes one, two and four.
jokes.setup: Homer's prayer of gratitude before surgery
jokes.punchline: Thanks God for banal pop culture items, skips volume three
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer prayer - mundane gratitude with inexplicably specific omission of volume three

jokes.text: My dad is very sick. What's gonna happen if he dies? Well, if he's been good, he'll go to heaven. In heaven, you get to do Whatever you like best all the time. Cloud goes up. Cloud goes down.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking about death, teacher explains heaven
jokes.punchline: Lisa imagines Homer in heaven still playing with up/down motion
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that shows Lisa understands her father's simple pleasures would continue in afterlife

jokes.text: Krusty, why are you here? Eh, it's part of my public service... for my glug-glug, vroom-vroom, thump-thump.
jokes.setup: Homer asking why Krusty is visiting sick children
jokes.punchline: Krusty's childish sound effects for his DUI crimes
jokes.explanation: Krusty's characteristic way of avoiding serious topics with childish euphemisms

jokes.text: You seem okay. Yeah? Well, I got news for ya. This ain't makeup.
jokes.setup: Homer commenting on Krusty's appearance
jokes.punchline: Krusty reveals his grotesque appearance is real, not clown makeup
jokes.explanation: Dark revelation about Krusty's health delivered with bitter humor

jokes.text: They say the greatest tragedy is when a father outlives his son. I've never fully understood why that is. Frankly, I can see an upside to it.
jokes.setup: Krusty discussing the tragedy of outliving children
jokes.punchline: Admits he sees benefits to children dying first
jokes.explanation: Shockingly dark humor that perfectly captures Krusty's cynical, selfish worldview

jokes.text: When I first heard about the operation, I was against it. But then I thought, if Homer wants to be a woman, so be it. Barney, I'm not getting a sex change! What? Well, what the hell am I supposed to do... with this jumbo thong bikini?
jokes.setup: Barney discussing Homer's operation
jokes.punchline: Reveals he thought it was sex change surgery and bought inappropriate gift
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection joke with Barney's characteristic confusion and inappropriate gift choice

jokes.text: Uh, Homer, I snuck you in a beer for old times' sake. Thanks, Moe. Uh, you know, Homer, that beer ain't free.
jokes.setup: Moe gives Homer a beer as a thoughtful gesture
jokes.punchline: Immediately demands payment, ruining the gesture
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe character moment - apparent kindness immediately undermined by greed

jokes.text: Mom, this is fascinating. Did you know that they'll stop Dad's heart for six whole minutes? I don't know if I like you reading all those cardiology books.
jokes.setup: Lisa studying heart surgery in medical texts
jokes.punchline: Marge's discomfort with Lisa's morbid fascination with the procedure
jokes.explanation: Shows Lisa's intellectual approach to scary situations contrasting with normal emotional response

jokes.text: And I've learned a great deal from this cow's heart I got at the butcher shop. Insert the retractor... and crank it until the ribs swing open like a rusty drawbridge.
jokes.setup: Lisa studying heart surgery with cow heart
jokes.punchline: Graphic medical instruction with disturbing imagery
jokes.explanation: Dark visual comedy with Lisa's inappropriate study materials and graphic description

jokes.text: Insert the retractor... and crank it until the ribs swing open like a rusty drawbridge. [Bones cracking] Ah! No! Blood! Ugh! Next, make an incision in the coronary artery-- And we are back with more of People Who Look Like Things.
jokes.setup: Lisa watching graphic surgery video with disturbing sound effects
jokes.punchline: Video gets taped over with silly talk show at crucial moment
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy with medical horror interrupted by banal television at worst moment

jokes.text: Oh, no! No! Someone taped over the end of this!
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to learn surgery from educational video
jokes.punchline: Critical medical information replaced by terrible TV show
jokes.explanation: Relatable frustration with taped-over videos at crucial moment

jokes.text: Oh, Homie, I could never remarry. Darn right. And to make sure, I want to be stuffed and put on the couch... as a constant reminder of our marital vows.
jokes.setup: Marge promising never to remarry if Homer dies
jokes.punchline: Homer wants to be taxidermied as furniture to enforce this
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and possessive Homer plan that's both romantic and disturbing

jokes.text: Bart, the saddest thing about this is I won't get to see you grow up... [Whispering] I know you're gonna turn out great, with or without your old man. Thanks, Dad. And Lisa... [Whispering] I guess this is the time to tell you-- [Whispering] you're adopted and I don't like you. Bart!
jokes.setup: Homer giving heartfelt final words to his children
jokes.punchline: Bart whispers cruel lie to Lisa in Homer's voice
jokes.explanation: Bart's prank during emotional moment perfectly fits his character while being genuinely mean

jokes.text: Bart, the saddest thing about this is I won't get to see you grow up, because-- [Whispering] I know you're gonna turn out great, with or without your old man.
jokes.setup: Homer giving heartfelt speech to Bart
jokes.punchline: Marge whispering corrections to his emotional farewell
jokes.explanation: Homer needing coaching for his deathbed speeches undermines the sentiment

jokes.text: Hi, everybody! Hi, Dr. Nick!
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick's entrance to the operating room
jokes.punchline: Everyone responds with his signature greeting
jokes.explanation: Establishes Dr. Nick's catchphrase that becomes iconic - simple but effective

jokes.text: These gloves came free with my toilet brush.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick putting on surgical gloves
jokes.punchline: Reveals the gloves' unsanitary origin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Nick moment - casual admission of completely inappropriate medical practice

jokes.text: Marge, this is Andre. Hello. [Chuckles] I think you two would make a lovely couple. My husband is still alive! Oh. Thank God. I hope he pulls through. Not me. [Chuckles]
jokes.setup: Patty introducing Marge to a potential replacement husband
jokes.punchline: Andre's honest disappointment that Homer might survive
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Andre's brutal honesty about hoping for Homer's death

jokes.text: Poor Mr. Homer. Could it be that my snack treats... are responsible for his wretched health? Gimme some jerky. Would you like some vodka with that? Oh, what the hell. Sure.
jokes.setup: Apu questioning if his unhealthy snacks caused Homer's problems
jokes.punchline: Immediately offers more unhealthy combinations without changing anything
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - momentary conscience immediately abandoned for business as usual

jokes.text: Doctor, what's wrong? Don't you know where to make the incision? All right, Nick. Don't panic. Think back to med school. Seriously, baby. I can prescribe anything I want. I know I'm supposed to cut something, but what? And where?
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick appearing lost during surgery
jokes.punchline: His medical school memory is about picking up women, not surgery
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dr. Nick incompetence - his med school flashback is about dating, not medicine

jokes.text: Hey! The incision in the coronary artery... must be made below the blockage! Below! Thanks, little girl!
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick completely lost during surgery
jokes.punchline: Lisa has to instruct the surgeon from her medical reading
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and role reversal - the child knows more than the doctor performing surgery

jokes.text: Well if it isn't my old friend Mr. McGreg... with a leg for an arm and an arm for a leg.
jokes.setup: Dr. Nick encountering a former patient
jokes.punchline: Casual greeting reveals he switched the man's limbs
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dr. Nick moment - casually refers to horrific malpractice as if greeting old friend. Extremely quotable structure.
